resposta:
solution: 
```python
from typing import List

class Solution:
    def memLeak(self, memory1: int, memory2: int) -> List[int]:
        i = 1
        while memory1 >= i and memory2 >= i:
            if memory1 >= memory2:
                memory1 -= i
            else:
                memory2 -= i
            i += 1
        return [i, memory1, memory2]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(sqrt(max(memory1, memory2)))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates through each second, allocating memory to the stick with more available memory. It stops when one of the sticks doesn't have enough memory, and returns the crash time and remaining memory.
